sections.lead: The Hermes AI agent features a simple, modular architecture with an AI core, accessible via CLI, API, or messaging gateway, and uses dual memory.

bullets.label: Access Methods
bullets.text: The agent can be accessed via a command-line interface (CLI), an API, or a gateway connecting to messaging services like Telegram, email, and Slack.

bullets.label: Core Components
bullets.text: The agent comes pre-installed with a suite of tools and skills.

bullets.label: Memory System
bullets.text: Hermes utilizes a dual memory system: internal memory (session transcripts, soul.md, user.md) and external memory (providers like MemZero).

sections.lead: Hermes' agentic loop processes user messages, building context, interacting with an LLM, executing tools, providing a response, and updating its memory.

bullets.label: Trigger
bullets.text: The core of Hermes operates on a straightforward agentic loop, which is triggered by every user message.

bullets.label: Context Building
bullets.text: Hermes builds its context by integrating internal memory and prompts, along with message history.

bullets.label: LLM Interaction
bullets.text: This context and message history are sent to the Large Language Model (LLM) for processing.

bullets.label: Tool Execution
bullets.text: The LLM decides whether to call and execute specific tools.

bullets.label: Memory Update
bullets.text: A crucial "memory update" step follows, where the agent analyzes the interaction to learn and store relevant information.

sections.lead: Hermes constructs its LLM context from specific markdown files, skill descriptions, and message history, using compression to manage token limits.

bullets.label: Core Context Files
bullets.text: Hermes builds context from `soul.md` for personality, `user.md` for user information, and `memory.md` for facts and workflows.

bullets.label: Additional Context
bullets.text: Descriptions of available skills and tools are also included in the context.

bullets.label: Message History
bullets.text: Recent message history contributes to the overall context.

bullets.label: Compression Trigger
bullets.text: Context compression is employed when the message history exceeds a set threshold, specifically 50% of the context window.

bullets.label: Compression Method
bullets.text: Previous messages are summarized and appended to the context, replacing the original verbose history, before each LLM turn or on context window errors.

sections.lead: The gateway enables Hermes to communicate across platforms, polling services, formatting messages, building conversation context, and managing user sessions.

bullets.label: Platform Connectivity
bullets.text: The gateway allows Hermes to communicate with users across multiple messaging platforms, including Telegram, WhatsApp, email, and Slack.

bullets.label: Operational Loop
bullets.text: It operates an asynchronous I/O loop that continuously polls these services for incoming messages.

bullets.label: Message Preparation
bullets.text: The gateway formats messages correctly and constructs the full conversation context, including message history from a local SQLite database.

bullets.label: Session Control
bullets.text: It features a session manager to handle message flow, enabling users to interrupt, steer, or queue commands.

sections.lead: Hermes manages memory through markdown files for persistent context, a SQLite database for session transcripts, and optional external providers for enhanced recall.

bullets.label: Persistent Context
bullets.text: Markdown files like `soul.md`, `memory.md`, and `user.md` provide persistent context for agent personality and learned facts.

bullets.label: Session Transcripts
bullets.text: A local SQLite database stores full transcripts of all user sessions, enabling efficient retrieval of conversation history.

bullets.label: Similarity Search
bullets.text: This database also maintains a bare text table specifically for similarity searches.

bullets.label: External Providers
bullets.text: Optional external memory providers, such as MemZero and Super Memory, can be integrated to enhance recall by dynamically querying for context.

sections.lead: Hermes supports cron jobs, an internal loop running every minute to schedule and execute automated tasks.

bullets.label: Functionality
bullets.text: Hermes supports cron jobs for scheduling automated tasks.

bullets.label: System Type
bullets.text: This system is an internal loop within Hermes.

bullets.label: Execution Frequency
bullets.text: The internal loop runs every minute to execute scheduled tasks.
